mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in total nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals expanding labor income and aggregate demand, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward 20-day forward returns after positive month-over-month PAYEMS changes are not significantly greater than zero (t-test p > 0.05) over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) exceeds 5.0%, restrictive monetary policy raises the risk of equity de-rating, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 5.0% during a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously rising consumer prices (CPIAUCSL) and rising unemployment (UNRATE) constitute a stagflationary regime that squeezes corporate margins and real household income, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between month-over-month changes in CPIAUCSL and UNRATE does not yield a negative and significant rank correlation (Rank IC < -0.03) with 20-day forward returns over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, month-over-month declines in industrial production (INDPRO) signal an impending growth slowdown that triggers de-risking, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: In late-cycle periods identified by elevated FEDFUNDS and low unemployment, INDPRO declines fail to precede negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in at least 60% of instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in retail and food services sales (RSAFS) indicates robust consumer demand momentum, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month RSAFS changes are not followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of instances over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in housing starts (HOUST) signals durable demand strength and construction sector optimism, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive changes in HOUST do not produce statistically significant positive 20-day forward returns (t-test p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a three-month window, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) exceeds the trailing year-over-year consumer price inflation rate (CPIAUCSL), positive real interest rates compress equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The FEDFUNDS-minus-CPIAUCSL-real-rate spread does not exhibit a negative and significant rank correlation (Rank IC < -0.03) with 5-day forward returns over any 2-year rolling window.
